overview: This week I built a PM Knowledge Assistant using Claude and ChromaDB to implement a full RAG pipeline, deployed apps to Streamlit, and began establishing evaluation baselines. I worked through Python 3.14/ChromaDB incompatibility, validated that retrieval grounding produces measurably better answers than generic model responses, and explored three retrieval quality techniques: hybrid search, re-ranking, and metadata filtering.

keyPoints.title: Cosine similarity scores were nearly 3x higher for semantically related queries
keyPoints.description: My first embedding script showed A vs B similarity at 0.4120 versus A vs C at 0.1437 — a 0.2684 difference achieved with zero keyword overlap.

keyPoints.title: RAG retrieved a specific 2.3% lift; without it, Claude gave generic benchmarks
keyPoints.description: The same model and prompt produced a document-grounded Q3 figure with RAG versus a useless industry range of 5–15% without it.

keyPoints.title: Claude synthesized a novel cross-document insight without being instructed to
keyPoints.description: When asked what's working in our personalization program, Claude independently connected behavioral targeting's 3:1 ratio to the signal-first architecture across two separate ingested documents.

keyPoints.title: Re-ranking with Claude Haiku correctly pruned irrelevant chunks that distance scores missed
keyPoints.description: Haiku dropped an Adobe Target chunk for a user persona query but kept it for an integration query — the same chunk judged correctly in both contexts.

keyPoints.title: Metadata filtering returned the correct document where distance scores chose wrong
keyPoints.description: A performance-category filter surfaced Q3 results at distance 0.662, while an unfiltered search would have preferred signal definitions at 0.590.

keyPoints.title: Python 3.14 and ChromaDB were incompatible, requiring a new 3.12 environment
keyPoints.description: ChromaDB's dependency on Pydantic v1 blocked Day 1 progress until I created a separate Python 3.12 environment to proceed.

keyPoints.title: Guardrails, not RAG alone, prevented Claude from hallucinating on missing context
keyPoints.description: When asked about Salesforce integration — absent from all ingested documents — Claude declined to answer only because my system prompt explicitly restricted it to retrieved context.
keyPoints.sectionName: RAG Grounds Information Retrieval, But Claude Still Hallucinates
